reason: Load the frontend checklist and route to the specific guidance files relevant to a global UI-preference feature.
The feature adds shared preference infrastructure and must stay consistent with the repo's app/feature/shared boundaries.
Header controls and page copy updates should follow existing client/server component boundaries.
Language/theme helpers should use lightweight shared hooks instead of scattering browser logic across pages.
Global persisted UI preferences need to follow the repo's Zustand and local state guidance.
Translation dictionaries and preference models should remain strongly typed.
The change affects accessibility, hydration behavior, and broad visible UI text coverage.
The translation/theme plumbing should be centralized and reused across pages rather than duplicated.
